name: MITRE.LIN.T1547.006.RULE Boot or Logon Autostart Execution: Kernel Modules and Extensions
notes:
The following auditd rules should be enabled:

-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S create_module,init_module,delete_module,get_kernel_syms,query_module,finit_module -F key=siem-kernel

Get more Linux MITRE rules: https://www.scnsoft.com/services/security/siem/linux-mitre-attack-rules
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Linux OS
when the event matches Linux Auditd Key (custom) is any of siem-kernel
when any of Linux UID (custom) are contained in any of MITRE: Linux Users - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
TD_list: APPLY MITRE.LIN.T1547.006.RULE Boot or Logon Autostart Execution: Kernel Modules and Extensions on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Linux OS
AND when the event matches Linux Auditd Key (custom) is any of siem-kernel
AND NOT when any of Linux UID (custom) are contained in any of MITRE: Linux Users - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
RED_list: Event name: MITRE.LIN.T1547.006.RULE Boot or Logon Autostart Execution: Kernel Modules and Extensions

Event description: Adversaries can use loadable kernel modules to covertly persist on a system and evade defenses.
See more on: https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1547/006
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
Include detected events by "Source IP" from this point forward, in the offense, for 0 second(s)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
